caseStudies.title: Embedded Systems Efficiency
caseStudies.description: A brief overview of case study 3.
caseStudies.content: Full content of Case Study 3 goes here.
caseStudies.overview: This project aimed to optimize embedded systems operating in resource-constrained environments, such as IoT devices and industrial controllers. The goal was to maximize performance and minimize memory and power consumption while maintaining system reliability.
caseStudies.problem: Devices were limited by small memory footprints and inefficient processing algorithms, leading to slow response times and occasional system instability. These issues hindered real-time data processing and reduced the overall reliability of the embedded solutions.
caseStudies.solution: The team refactored critical algorithms for efficiency, reduced memory usage through data structure optimization, and improved real-time execution by prioritizing time-sensitive tasks. Profiling tools were used to identify bottlenecks, and unnecessary background processes were eliminated.

results.summary: These enhancements extended device lifespans and reduced maintenance costs, allowing the client to defer hardware refresh cycles by over two years.

caseStudies.title: OTA Firmware Update Platform
caseStudies.description: Secure, reliable over-the-air firmware delivery at scale.
caseStudies.overview: The client needed a robust OTA update platform capable of delivering firmware to hundreds of thousands of field-deployed devices with varying connectivity. The platform had to ensure cryptographic integrity, support rollback, and minimize bandwidth consumption.
caseStudies.problem: Firmware updates were distributed manually via USB by field technicians, costing thousands of hours per release cycle. Devices in remote locations went months without critical security patches, increasing vulnerability exposure significantly.
caseStudies.solution: A delta-compression OTA platform was developed with end-to-end signing using hardware security modules. The system supports staged rollouts, automatic rollback on failure, and bandwidth-adaptive chunked transfers. A device management dashboard provides real-time fleet update visibility.

results.summary: Security patches now reach the entire fleet within 48 hours of release. The elimination of manual updates freed up field engineering resources for higher-value work.

caseStudies.title: Real-Time Telemetry Analytics
caseStudies.description: Streaming telemetry from vehicles to actionable fleet insights.
caseStudies.overview: Vehicle sensor data from a commercial fleet was being stored but rarely analyzed in time to prevent failures. The goal was to build a real-time analytics layer that could surface anomalies and trigger maintenance alerts before breakdowns occurred.
caseStudies.problem: Terabytes of raw telemetry data were archived daily but queried only in retrospective audits after incidents. Predictive maintenance was impossible without a streaming layer, leading to costly unplanned downtime averaging 14 hours per incident.
caseStudies.solution: A streaming analytics pipeline was deployed to process vehicle telemetry in real time. Machine learning models were trained on historical failure patterns and deployed at the edge to score each vehicle's health continuously. Alerts were routed to maintenance teams via a mobile dashboard.

results.summary: Fleet operators now intervene proactively before failures occur. The reduction in unplanned downtime directly improved on-time delivery rates and customer satisfaction scores.
